facts: Our Sun emits light across the ENTIRE visible spectrum (rainbow colors).
Its peak intensity is in the GREEN-BLUE portion of the spectrum.
When all these wavelengths combine, the Sun's light appears PURE WHITE from space.
Earth's atmosphere scatters blue light, making the Sun appear yellow/orange to us!
idea.payoff: Your crayon box lied: The Sun is actually a WHITE star!
idea.title: The Sun's TRUE Color! ☀️🤯

scenes.visual: A stylized yellow sun graphic. As text appears, the sun briefly flickers or a question mark icon pulses over it, creating a surprising effect.
A white light source (representing the sun) expanding into a full visible light spectrum (rainbow colors). The spectrum arcs around the central text.

scenes.text: Peak is green-blue. From space, it's PURE WHITE!
scenes.visual: First, a spectrum graph with a clear peak highlighted in green-blue. This transitions to a stunning, brilliant pure white sun against a dark, star-filled space backdrop.

scenes.text: Earth's atmosphere makes it look YELLOW/ORANGE!
scenes.visual: A pure white sun. An Earth graphic with a distinct blue atmospheric glow slides in from the side. As the Earth's atmosphere 'filters' the sun's light, the sun behind it visually shifts to a yellow/orange hue.

scenes.text: Your crayon box lied: The Sun is actually a WHITE star!
scenes.visual: A bold, radiant pure white star dominating the screen. Perhaps a stylized yellow crayon with a red 'X' over it briefly appears, replaced by the white star, reinforcing the payoff.
